verifyNote: Confirmed Brazil vs Morocco, Group C (Match 7), 6:00 PM ET via MetLife Stadium official events page and NY/NJ Host Committee schedule (post Dec 5 2025 final draw).

hype.label: Unmissable
hype.blurb: Five-time champ Brazil opens against the team that humiliated them 2-1 in Tangier in 2023, in front of the loudest neutral-but-not-neutral crowd of the group stage. If you can get in, you go.

factors.v: Tournament-opening Group C statement game; both expect to advance, neither wants to drop points
Heavy Brazilian samba presence plus a famously thunderous Moroccan traveling support in the NY metro
Revenge angle: Morocco beat Brazil 2-1 in 2023 after their 2022 World Cup semifinal run
Vinicius Jr and Rodrygo vs Hakimi, Bounou and Brahim Diaz: elite end to end

preview.summary: Brazil arrive as perennial favorites chasing a sixth star, but Morocco are no longer anyone's underdog after reaching the 2022 semifinals and beating Brazil in a 2023 friendly. This is a heavyweight Group C opener where the loser instantly faces qualification pressure. Expect tactical caution early, then fireworks.
preview.homeForm: Brazil come in as one of the top-ranked sides, rebuilt around pace and attacking flair after a bumpy qualifying stretch.
preview.awayForm: Morocco are the most dangerous non-traditional power in the tournament, organized, fast in transition, and battle-tested on big stages.
preview.homeKey: Vinicius Jr: game-breaking dribbler who can settle it in one moment
preview.awayKey: Achraf Hakimi: marauding right back who turns defense into attack
storylines: Can Morocco repeat the 2023 upset, or was that a one-off against a transitioning Brazil side?
Vinicius Jr and Rodrygo finally given a stage to define a tournament from match one
Bounou vs Brazil's front line: the goalkeeper who broke Spanish hearts in 2022
preview.h2h: Morocco stunned Brazil 2-1 in a March 2023 friendly in Tangier, their most famous result against the Selecao.
preview.prediction: Brazil edge a tense, end-to-end 2-1, but Morocco make them sweat into the final ten minutes and the neutral fan leaves having gotten their money's worth.

tickets.sellout: Effectively sold out at face value; resale-only for most buyers and prices have climbed well above FIFA's original $60-$620 group-stage estimate.
tickets.bestValue: Category 4 / upper-tier corners (cheapest official tier, best price-to-view ratio)
tickets.atmosphere: Lower-bowl behind the goals and the upper-tier ends, where Brazilian and Moroccan supporter groups cluster
tickets.note: Resale prices ($1,150 and up across TickPick, Goal, Vivid Seats, StubHub) are estimates that move fast; buy from verified resale and target the upper ends for noise over a quieter pricey midfield seat.
